behaviors.description: Conversation history is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages before the LLM call when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is at module wiring: history selection + prompt assembly + LLM client payload. A unit test of only the trimming helper can miss ordering/serialization bugs in the actual request path (MOCK-MISS). No OS/network realism is required if the LLM call is intercepted locally.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real request-building path with a 15+ message history, intercept the outbound LLM payload, and assert only the latest 10 messages (correct order, roles, and content) are sent.

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer’s specific question instead of being generic/off-topic.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality, which is non-deterministic and can vary run-to-run (LLM-DEP). Mocking would hide the core failure (MOCK-HIDE) because a stub can always return an on-topic answer.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed evaluation set of customer questions and call the real model. Score responses for relevance (LLM-judge rubric or human-labeled comparator), require a pass-rate threshold, and track variance across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal response for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: If tone classification is model-driven, correctness depends on real model judgment (LLM-DEP). A mocked checker can always return "too formal" and mask real misclassification risk (MOCK-HIDE). Multi-step orchestration exists, but the key failure here is the model decision itself.
behaviors.test_description: Provide casual-tier scenarios with intentionally formal first drafts, run the real tone-check step, and assert it flags too-formal cases and causes a regeneration call. Measure detection rate over a labeled set.

behaviors.description: User message, first draft, any regenerated draft, and final served response are persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a persistence and data-model correctness boundary across app modules and DB writes. Pure mocks can miss schema/transaction/timestamp mapping issues (MOCK-MISS). Real external infra is not required if using a real local test DB.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a chat turn (including forced regeneration path) against a real test database, then query stored records to verify all exchange artifacts exist, are linked to the same conversation, and timestamps are populated and ordered correctly.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, only the second response is exposed to the customer; rejected draft is never shown.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is orchestration between generation, tone gate, and response delivery. A unit test of replacement logic alone can miss wiring/race/order issues in the end-to-end handler (MOCK-MISS). No real LLM quality dependency is required if outputs are controlled.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full handler with deterministic first/second model outputs where the first is rejected by tone gate; assert the returned API payload/stream to the customer contains only the regenerated text, while the first draft is retained only in internal logs.
